channel.title: Prince Caleb — Technical Archive
channel.description: Articles on web development, freelancing, and building software in Ghana.

item.title: How I Used Three LLMs to Power One Live Chat: The Gemini, OpenRouter, and Groq Failover Chain Behind This Site
item.description: The live chat on this website runs on three LLM providers at once — Gemini, OpenRouter, and Groq — not for cleverness, but for uptime. A case study on building a failover chain where a single conversation survives any provider going down.

item.title: How I Connected Three (3) LLM-Powered Live Chat to Gmail, Slack, Google Sheets, and WhatsApp With Composio
item.description: A practical case study on turning a website live chat from a simple conversation widget into an action-taking assistant that can qualify leads, book calls, and notify Gmail, Slack, Google Sheets, and WhatsApp through Composio.

item.title: AI Booking Assistants for Salons in Accra: Cutting No-Shows With Automation
item.description: A no-show costs a stylist a paid hour with nothing to show for it. An AI booking assistant that reminds, confirms, and reschedules cuts that loss without an awkward conversation.

item.title: Using AI to Match Volunteers to Causes: A Guide for NGOs in Ghana
item.description: Matching a volunteer's actual skills to where they're needed usually happens by memory. AI matching does it systematically, at a scale memory can't handle.

item.title: AI Chatbots for Microfinance: Answering Loan Questions Instantly
item.description: Loan applicants calling to ask "am I eligible" or "what documents do I need" can get an instant answer from a chatbot, freeing loan officers for actual underwriting.

item.title: AI-Assisted Document Review for Law Firms in Ghana
item.description: AI document review doesn't replace a lawyer's judgment on a case. It replaces the hours spent manually scanning contracts for the clauses that matter.

item.title: AI Tutors and Chat Support for Schools and Training Centers in Accra
item.description: An AI tutor doesn't replace a teacher — it answers the question a student is too embarrassed to ask again in class.

item.title: Using AI to Predict Delivery Delays for Logistics Companies in Ghana
item.description: Telling a customer "your delivery might be late" before it happens builds more trust than apologizing after it already is.

item.title: AI Chatbots for Restaurants: Handling Reservations and FAQs in Accra
item.description: A restaurant's phone rings constantly with the same three questions. An AI chatbot on WhatsApp can answer them while staff focus on the dining room.

item.title: AI-Powered Product Recommendations for Retail Stores in Ghana
item.description: Generic "customers also bought" recommendations barely move sales. Recommendations tuned to actual local buying patterns do more, but need real data to work.

item.title: Using AI to Qualify Real Estate Leads Before They Reach an Agent
item.description: Not every inquiry is a serious buyer. AI lead qualification sorts them before an agent spends time on a conversation that was never going anywhere.

item.title: AI Chat Assistants for Clinics: Answering Patient Questions 24/7 in Accra
item.description: Patients ask the same handful of questions constantly — hours, services, insurance, directions. An AI assistant answers them at 11pm without waking up a receptionist.

item.title: Booking Apps for Salons and Spas: What Ghanaian Businesses Should Know
item.description: Building a booking app is the easy part. Getting clients to actually download one for a single salon is the part most owners underestimate.

item.title: Building a Volunteer Coordination App for NGOs in Ghana
item.description: Coordinating volunteers through a WhatsApp broadcast list works for one event. It stops working the moment an NGO runs multiple ongoing programs.

item.title: Mobile Banking Apps for Microfinance Institutions in Accra
item.description: A mobile banking app for an MFI isn't a luxury feature — it's often the difference between a customer staying and one switching to a mobile money wallet instead.

item.title: Building a Client Portal App for Law Firms in Ghana
item.description: Clients calling to ask "any updates on my case" is a symptom — a client portal treats the actual problem, which is a lack of visibility.

item.title: Mobile Apps for Schools: Parent Communication and Fee Payments
item.description: Parent-teacher communication in most Ghanaian schools happens through a WhatsApp group that becomes unreadable within a week. A dedicated app fixes that without losing the immediacy.

item.title: Building a Delivery Tracking App for Logistics Companies in Accra
item.description: Customers don't want a tracking app — they want to know where their package is. Sometimes a link does that better than an app ever could.

item.title: Building a Table Reservation and Loyalty App for Restaurants in Ghana
item.description: Restaurant apps fail for the same reason most apps fail: nobody has a reason to open them more than once. Reservations and loyalty rewards give people that reason.

item.title: Mobile Loyalty Apps for Retail Businesses in Accra
item.description: A loyalty app only works if customers open it more than once — which means the reward has to be worth more than the download.
